title: Context Graph
description: Portable compressed conversation state graph produced by context-graph-compressor v2.

mode.description: Compression mode used to generate this graph.

desc.description: One-sentence description of the conversation topic and current state.

chunks.description: Number of chunks used during chunked compression. Present only when chunked compression was applied.

meta.description: Reserved for future extensibility. Parsers should ignore unknown fields.

n.description: Array of context nodes representing key topics, decisions, problems, goals, code, assumptions, and open threads.

rel.description: Explicit relationships between nodes. Used for cross-cutting dependencies that parent-child nesting cannot express.

handoff.description: 2-3 line plain-English summary of current state. Paste this into a new chat along with the full JSON to resume the session.

id.description: Unique node identifier. Format: n1, n2, n1.1 (child), n1.1.1 (grandchild).

t.description: Node type. F=Fact, D=Decision, P=Problem, G=Goal, C=Code, A=Assumption, X=Context/open thread.

i.description: Importance level. h=high (losing this breaks continuity), m=medium (useful context), l=low (dropped in compact mode).

st.description: Status of this node. Omit if not applicable (e.g. for plain facts).

s.description: Telegraphic summary. Max 1-2 sentences in compact mode. Code identifiers must be verbatim.

conf.description: Confidence score. Only present on inferred/assumption nodes. 0.9-1.0=explicit, 0.7-0.9=implied, <0.7=uncertain (node should not exist).

c.description: Child nodes. Use for details that belong under this topic. For cross-cutting dependencies, use top-level rel array instead.
